pattern: A chunk boundary is legal wherever the maximum of the left prefix is no greater than the minimum of the right suffix.
hints: You may cut between positions i and i+1 only if everything on the left is at most everything on the right.
Precompute prefix maximums and suffix minimums, then a boundary is valid when prefixMax[i] <= suffixMin[i+1].
Equivalently keep a monotonic stack of chunk maximums: while the next value is smaller, merge chunks by collapsing the stack down to the largest blocking maximum.

edge_inputs.description: strictly decreasing forces a single chunk
near sorted with a duplicate tail
already sorted with duplicates splits between distinct runs
equal values must not be split apart incorrectly
large gap value early blocks several boundaries
value repeated across a potential boundary

stress.description: random arrays with a small value range so duplicates and merges are common
stress.note: narrow value range stresses the duplicate-handling against the prefix-max suffix-min boundary
